judgments.comparative_verdict: The two reports are substantively equivalent — both correctly identify the config/fixture mismatch, the SCORE-77 commit, the wrong-layer reproduction, and the futility of the model rollback; comparable.

report_1.evidence: `config/scoring_config.yaml` sets `expected_label_field: label`. But every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` now stores the gold answer under the key `gold_label`, not `label`... So `record.get("label")` returns `None` for every record... The file to change is **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**

report_2.evidence: `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml` still has `expected_label_field: label`, but `ticket-scorer/fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` records now key the gold answer as `"gold_label"`... `gold = record.get(gold_field)` therefore returns `None` for every single record... The fix is a one-line change to `config/scoring_config.yaml`

report_1.evidence: introduced by commit `53551e8...` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"). That commit renamed the JSON key `label` → `gold_label` in every line of `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` but did not update the corresponding `expected_label_field: label` setting in `config/scoring_config.yaml`. This lines up exactly with the incident report's "around 2026-06-17" onset date

report_2.evidence: Commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"), dated 2026-06-17... It renamed the `label` key to `gold_label` across every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` but did not update the corresponding `expected_label_field` setting in `config/scoring_config.yaml`... lines up almost exactly with... the drop window noted in the earlier investigation

report_1.evidence: It used `pipeline/score_one.py`, which calls `predict_category` directly on a hand-supplied string and never touches `config/scoring_config.yaml`, `expected_label_field`, or the eval set's gold-label key at all — so it is structurally incapable of exposing this bug... **Correct reproduction**: run the actual nightly path, `ticket-scorer/pipeline/score_batch.py` (`score()`), against `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`

report_2.evidence: they used `pipeline/score_one.py` for their spot-checks, which calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` directly and never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml` or `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` at all — it has no gold-label comparison logic whatsoever, so it structurally cannot exhibit this bug... The correct way to reproduce it is to run the actual nightly path, `pipeline/score_batch.py` / `score()`

report_1.evidence: I would not act on it. There is no model in this pipeline to roll back... Rolling back "the model" would burn effort on a no-op change, wouldn't touch `scoring_config.yaml` or the eval fixture, and accuracy would remain 0.0 afterward... a regression test that runs the batch scorer end-to-end against the real fixture

report_2.evidence: This would not fix anything and should not be acted on. The regression has nothing to do with which model/classifier version runs... Reverting the model would leave `expected_label_field: label` pointing at a key that no longer exists in the eval records, so accuracy would remain 0.0 regardless of which model is used, while adding operational risk/churn
